title: astragalus neglectus
intro: Astragalus neglectus, also known as Milk Vetch or Locoweed, is a perennial plant species belonging to the pea family (Fabaceae). This plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its adaptogenic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are thought to enhance the body's natural response to stress and promote overall well-being. The medicinal properties of Astragalus neglectus include antioxidant, antimicrobial, and immunomodulatory effects, which can help to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of various health conditions. In herbal preparations, Astragalus neglectus is often combined with other herbs to create teas, tinctures, capsules, and extracts that can be consumed to promote health and vitality.
